milestones.title: Further optimize Haiku 4.5 synthesis speed (partially addressed, still 7-14s on complex queries)
milestones.description: The Haiku 4.5 synthesis step in the two-stage reasoning pipeline takes 7-14 seconds on complex clinical queries. This is the main bottleneck — Sonnet reasoning is fast (2-4s) but synthesis lags. Investigate prompt length reduction, token limits, and whether streaming the synthesis response can reduce perceived wait time.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the Evidence Engine repo, further optimize the Haiku 4.5 synthesis step in src/lib/bedrock-converse.ts. Initial token reduction was done in PR #43 but complex queries still take 7-14s. Investigate streaming the response, prompt restructuring, or alternative models. Target: under 5s for typical queries.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Optimize Evidence Engine Synthesis Speed

**Repo:** sevaro-evidence-engine
**Key files:** src/lib/bedrock-converse.ts, src/lib/evidence-tools.ts

## Context
The two-stage reasoning pipeline uses Sonnet 4.6 for reasoning (2-4s) then Haiku 4.5 for synthesis (7-14s). The synthesis step is the bottleneck.

## Investigation areas
1. Measure prompt token count going to Haiku — are we sending too much context?
2. Check if KB results can be trimmed before synthesis
3. Evaluate streaming the synthesis response to reduce perceived latency
4. Consider if Haiku 4.5 is the right model or if a faster option exists

## Acceptance criteria
- Typical queries synthesize in under 5 seconds
- Complex queries under 8 seconds
- No quality regression in synthesis output
- Run qa/run_smoke.sh to verify
manualSteps: Open src/lib/bedrock-converse.ts and find the synthesis function
Add timing logs around the Haiku invocation
Check the prompt token count being sent

milestones.title: Improve PubMed query construction (0 results on valid topics)
milestones.description: The live PubMed search tool sometimes returns 0 results for clearly valid clinical topics. The query construction in pubmed-search.ts needs better term mapping, MeSH term expansion, and fallback strategies when initial queries fail.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the Evidence Engine repo, fix PubMed query construction in src/lib/pubmed-search.ts. Some valid clinical topics return 0 results. Add MeSH term fallbacks and broader query strategies.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Fix PubMed Query Construction

**Repo:** sevaro-evidence-engine
**Key file:** src/lib/pubmed-search.ts

## Problem
Sonnet generates search queries for PubMed via the search_pubmed tool, but some valid topics return 0 results. Examples: rare neurological conditions, newer treatments.

## Fix approach
1. Add MeSH term expansion — map common clinical terms to MeSH vocabulary
2. Implement query fallback: if first query returns 0, broaden terms and retry
3. Consider splitting compound queries into simpler sub-queries
4. Log failed queries for future analysis

## Acceptance criteria
- Known-failing queries now return relevant results
- No increase in irrelevant results for queries that already work
- Run qa/run_smoke.sh to verify
manualSteps: Add MeSH term mapping for common clinical terms
Add fallback query logic when results are 0

milestones.title: Deploy document-processor + kb-sync Lambdas for automated KB updates
milestones.description: Currently the Knowledge Base (1,472 docs) is updated manually by uploading files to S3. Build two Lambda functions: document-processor (fetches new content from PubMed/FDA/ClinicalTrials, formats for KB) and kb-sync (triggers KB re-ingestion after new docs land in S3).
milestones.quickPrompt: In the Evidence Engine repo, create two Lambda functions: document-processor (fetches new clinical content from PubMed/FDA, formats for KB ingestion) and kb-sync (triggers Bedrock KB re-ingestion). Deploy with SAM.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Automated KB Update Pipeline

**Repo:** sevaro-evidence-engine
**New files:** lambda/document-processor/, lambda/kb-sync/

## Context
The Bedrock KB (T4W8S8RNMN) has ~1,472 docs in S3 sources/external/active/. Currently updated manually.

## Build
1. document-processor Lambda: Scheduled daily, fetches new content from PubMed, FDA, ClinicalTrials APIs, formats as text files, uploads to S3
2. kb-sync Lambda: Triggered by S3 events, calls Bedrock StartIngestionJob to re-index
3. SAM template for deployment
4. EventBridge rule for daily schedule

## Acceptance criteria
- New docs appear in S3 after Lambda runs
- KB re-ingests automatically
- No duplicate documents
- CloudWatch logs for monitoring
manualSteps: Deploy with sam build && sam deploy
Verify KB re-ingestion in Bedrock console

milestones.title: Live voice streaming during encounters
milestones.description: Currently transcription only works post-recording. Add real-time voice streaming during clinical encounters so clinicians see words appearing as they speak. This requires WebSocket integration with a streaming transcription service.
prerequisites: AWS Transcribe or Deepgram API access
milestones.quickPrompt: In the OPSAmple repo, add real-time voice streaming during encounters. Currently post-recording only. Use WebSocket streaming to show words as they're spoken.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Real-Time Voice Streaming

**Repo:** OPSAmplehtml
**Key area:** Transcription components

## Context
OPSAmple has post-recording transcription but no live streaming. Clinicians want to see words appear in real-time during encounters.

## Approach
1. Add WebSocket connection to streaming transcription service
2. Display interim results in real-time, replace with finals
3. Handle microphone permissions and audio capture in browser
4. Consider AWS Transcribe Medical for HIPAA compliance

## Acceptance criteria
- Words appear within 500ms of being spoken
- Final transcription matches post-recording quality
- Works on Chrome and Safari
- Graceful fallback if streaming unavailable

milestones.title: Fix voice handoff reliability (phrase matching drift)
milestones.description: Voice handoff during workouts sometimes fails to match spoken phrases due to transcription variations. The phrase matching and normalization logic needs improvement to handle more speech patterns reliably.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the Workouts repo, fix voice handoff reliability. Phrase matching drifts with transcription variations. Improve normalization and matching logic.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Fix Voice Handoff Reliability

**Repo:** Workouts

## Problem
Voice handoff phrase matching is unreliable — transcription variations cause missed handoffs. Users say 'next exercise' but transcription might produce 'next excersize' or 'necks exercise'.

## Fix approach
1. Review current phrase matching in voice-handoff.ts
2. Add fuzzy matching or Levenshtein distance tolerance
3. Normalize common transcription errors
4. Add touch fallback for all critical voice actions
5. Test with varied speech patterns

## Acceptance criteria
- Voice handoff succeeds >95% of the time
- Touch fallback available for every voice action
- Test with qa/TEST_RUNBOOK.md voice scenarios

milestones.title: Build OTP email verification via SES + RDS
milestones.description: Replace Supabase Auth OTP with a custom solution using Amazon SES for email delivery and RDS for OTP code storage. This is the last Supabase dependency in the iOS app.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the neuro-plans-v2 repo, build OTP email verification using Amazon SES for delivery and RDS for code storage. Create /api/auth/send-otp and /api/auth/verify-otp endpoints.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Custom OTP Email Verification

**Repo:** neuro-plans-v2

## Context
The iOS app currently uses Supabase Auth for OTP email verification. This is the last Supabase dependency. Replace with SES + RDS.

## Build
1. POST /api/auth/send-otp — generate 6-digit code, store in RDS with expiry, send via SES
2. POST /api/auth/verify-otp — validate code against RDS, return success/failure
3. Rate limiting: max 5 attempts per email per hour
4. Code expiry: 10 minutes

## Acceptance criteria
- Email arrives within 30 seconds
- Code validates correctly
- Expired codes are rejected
- Rate limiting prevents abuse
manualSteps: Set up SES for sending from your domain
Create OTP codes table in RDS
Update iOS app to call new endpoints

milestones.title: Decide BYOK keep/remove after App Store approval
milestones.description: After v1.0.1 is approved, decide whether to keep the Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) feature for OpenAI or remove it to simplify the app. BYOK lets users enter their own API key instead of using the edge function proxy.
milestones.quickPrompt: Review the RepGenius BYOK feature and decide whether to keep or remove it. If removing, clean up SettingsView.swift and API key storage.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: BYOK Decision

**Repo:** RepGenius

## Context
RepGenius supports BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) where users can enter their own OpenAI API key. The edge function proxy is the default. BYOK adds complexity but gives power users control.

## Decision factors
- Does BYOK add enough value to justify maintenance?
- Does it complicate App Store review (AI data practices)?
- Would removing it simplify the settings UI?

## If removing
1. Remove API key input from SettingsView.swift
2. Remove UserDefaults key storage
3. Simplify WorkoutGenerationService to always use edge function
4. Submit update to App Store
manualSteps: Review BYOK usage analytics if available

milestones.title: Core Data migration from UserDefaults
milestones.description: Migrate workout history storage from UserDefaults to Core Data for persistent, queryable workout history. UserDefaults is not designed for large datasets and has size limits.
milestones.quickPrompt: In RepGenius, migrate workout history from UserDefaults to Core Data. Create Core Data model, migration logic, and update all reads/writes.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Core Data Migration

**Repo:** RepGenius

## Context
Workout history is stored in UserDefaults as encoded JSON. This works for small amounts but won't scale. Core Data provides proper persistence, querying, and migration support.

## Build
1. Create Core Data model (.xcdatamodeld) with Workout, Exercise, Set entities
2. Write migration logic to move existing UserDefaults data to Core Data
3. Update WorkoutHistoryService to use Core Data
4. Add Core Data stack to app lifecycle
5. Test migration with existing data

## Acceptance criteria
- Existing workout history preserved after migration
- New workouts save to Core Data
- Query performance is fast (< 100ms for history list)
- UserDefaults workout data cleaned up after successful migration

1. Run both tasks on headset
2. Verify IMU data capture (acceleration, rotation)
3. Check gait metrics (step count, cadence, symmetry)
4. Compare to manual measurement

## Acceptance criteria
- Both tasks run without crashes
- IMU data captures reliably
- Gait metrics are clinically plausible

milestones.title: Clinical threshold tuning with real patient data
milestones.description: Tune clinical thresholds (normal/abnormal boundaries) using real patient data instead of synthetic test data. Requires collecting baseline data from healthy controls and patients with known conditions.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the SDNE repo, tune clinical thresholds using real patient data. Collect baselines from healthy controls and compare to patients with known conditions.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Clinical Threshold Tuning

**Repo:** SDNE

## Context
Current thresholds are based on literature values and synthetic data. Need real patient data to calibrate.

## Steps
1. Collect baseline data from 10+ healthy controls
2. Collect data from patients with known speech/motor conditions
3. Analyze distributions and set clinically meaningful thresholds
4. Update threshold configuration
5. Validate with held-out data

## Acceptance criteria
- Thresholds based on real clinical data
- Sensitivity/specificity acceptable for screening
- Dashboard displays normal/abnormal correctly

milestones.title: Watch IMU integration for motor tasks
milestones.description: Integrate Apple Watch IMU data for motor and gait tasks (T09 finger tapping, T10 hand movements, T11 pronation-supination, T15/T16 gait). The watch provides higher-quality wrist/hand movement data than the headset.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the SDNE repo, integrate Apple Watch IMU data for motor tasks T09, T10, T11, T15, T16. Capture accelerometer and gyroscope data from the watch during tasks.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Watch IMU Integration

**Repo:** SDNE

## Tasks requiring Watch data
- T09: Finger tapping
- T10: Hand movements
- T11: Pronation-supination
- T15/T16: Gait

## Build
1. Watch app for IMU data capture
2. BLE sync between watch and headset/phone
3. Time-sync IMU data with task timestamps
4. Upload watch data alongside headset data
5. Combined analysis in Lambda

## Acceptance criteria
- Watch captures IMU at 100Hz during tasks
- Data syncs with headset task timing
- Combined metrics available in dashboard
manualSteps: Create watchOS app with IMU capture

milestones.title: Note generation pipeline polish (120s Lambda timeout)
milestones.description: The note generation Lambda has a 120-second timeout which is occasionally hit on complex encounters. Polish the pipeline to be more reliable — optimize prompt sizes, add progress feedback, and handle timeout gracefully.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome repo, polish the note generation pipeline. Optimize for the 120s Lambda timeout — reduce prompt sizes, add progress feedback, handle timeouts gracefully.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Note Generation Polish

**Repo:** SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome

## Problem
The generate-note Lambda occasionally times out at 120s on complex encounters. Need to optimize.

## Fix approach
1. Measure current prompt token counts
2. Trim unnecessary context from prompts
3. Add progress feedback to UI (generating... section 1/3...)
4. Graceful timeout handling — save partial results
5. Consider splitting into multiple Lambda calls for very long encounters

## Acceptance criteria
- No timeouts on typical encounters
- Progress feedback visible in UI
- Partial results saved on timeout
manualSteps: Check Lambda logs for timeout frequency

milestones.title: Chart prep to visit transition workflow
milestones.description: When a clinician finishes chart prep and starts a visit, the extension should smoothly transition — carrying forward chart prep notes and switching to encounter recording mode.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome repo, build the chart prep to visit transition workflow. Carry forward chart prep notes into the encounter and switch to recording mode.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Chart Prep to Visit Transition

**Repo:** SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome

## Workflow
1. Clinician completes chart prep (reviews prior notes, labs, imaging)
2. Patient arrives — clinician clicks 'Start Visit'
3. Extension carries forward chart prep context
4. Switches to encounter recording mode
5. Chart prep data available as reference during encounter

## Build
1. 'Start Visit' button in chart prep view
2. Context transfer from chart prep to encounter
3. Split-view or reference panel during encounter
4. Ensure recording captures only the encounter, not chart prep

## Acceptance criteria
- Smooth transition from chart prep to visit
- Chart prep notes accessible during encounter
- Recording starts fresh for the encounter
manualSteps: Add Start Visit button to ChartPrepView

milestones.title: Wire up Deepgram streaming (implemented but not connected)
milestones.description: The DeepgramService class is fully implemented but never instantiated — streaming transcription is not wired up. Currently uses batch transcription via Lambda. Connect the streaming service for real-time transcription during encounters.
prerequisites: Deepgram API key with streaming access
milestones.quickPrompt: In the SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome repo, wire up the existing DeepgramService for real-time streaming transcription. The class is implemented but not instantiated anywhere.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Wire Up Deepgram Streaming

**Repo:** SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome

## Context
DeepgramService.ts is fully implemented with WebSocket subprotocol auth. But it's never instantiated — the extension uses batch transcription via Lambda /transcribe endpoint.

## Wire up
1. Instantiate DeepgramService in the offscreen document
2. Connect microphone audio to Deepgram WebSocket
3. Display interim results in real-time in the side panel
4. Replace final transcription with Deepgram finals
5. Keep batch transcription as fallback

## Acceptance criteria
- Words appear in real-time during recording
- Final transcript quality matches or exceeds batch
- Fallback to batch if streaming fails
- No memory leaks from WebSocket connections
manualSteps: Find where to instantiate it in offscreen.ts
Wire interim results to side panel display

milestones.title: Amazon Transcribe Medical for HIPAA compliance
milestones.description: Replace or supplement Deepgram with Amazon Transcribe Medical for HIPAA-compliant transcription. The Lambda endpoint already exists (sevaro-transcribe-staging) but needs to be integrated into the extension.
prerequisites: Deepgram streaming wired up (to compare quality)
milestones.quickPrompt: In the SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome repo, integrate Amazon Transcribe Medical for HIPAA-compliant transcription. The Lambda endpoint exists at sevaro-transcribe-staging.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Amazon Transcribe Medical Integration

**Repo:** SevaroNeuro-scribe-chrome

## Context
Deepgram is not HIPAA-covered without an Enterprise BAA. AWS Transcribe Medical is covered under the existing AWS BAA.

## Build
1. Create TranscriptionService that uses sevaro-transcribe-staging Lambda
2. Support both streaming (WebSocket) and batch modes
3. Compare quality with Deepgram Nova-3 Medical
4. Make transcription provider configurable

## Acceptance criteria
- HIPAA-compliant transcription available
- Quality comparable to Deepgram
- Provider switchable via config
- No PHI sent to non-HIPAA services

milestones.title: Test production Cognito auth flow (DEV_MODE=true bypasses all auth)
milestones.description: DEV_MODE is true in constants.ts which bypasses all Cognito authentication. The auth flow is implemented but has never been tested in production mode. Set DEV_MODE=false and verify the full login/logout cycle works.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the sevaro-scribe repo, set DEV_MODE=false in src/constants.ts and test the full Cognito auth flow. Fix any issues that surface.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Test Cognito Auth

**Repo:** sevaro-scribe

## Context
DEV_MODE=true in constants.ts bypasses all auth. The Cognito popup login flow is implemented but untested in production mode.

## Steps
1. Set DEV_MODE=false in src/constants.ts
2. Load extension in Chrome
3. Click extension icon — should show login popup
4. Log in with steve@sevaro.com
5. Verify authenticated state persists
6. Test logout
7. Fix any auth flow issues

## Acceptance criteria
- Login popup appears when not authenticated
- Login succeeds with valid credentials
- Authenticated state persists across popup open/close
- Logout clears state
- Extension features only accessible when authenticated

milestones.title: Migrate from Deepgram Nova-3 to Amazon Transcribe Medical (HIPAA)
milestones.description: Replace Deepgram Nova-3 streaming with Amazon Transcribe Medical for HIPAA compliance. The Lambda endpoint (/get-credentials) already exists to vend temporary credentials.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the sevaro-scribe repo, replace Deepgram Nova-3 streaming with Amazon Transcribe Medical. Use the existing /get-credentials Lambda endpoint for temp credentials.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Migrate to Transcribe Medical

**Repo:** sevaro-scribe

## Context
Currently uses Deepgram Nova-3 via WebSocket subprotocol auth. Need HIPAA-compliant alternative.

## Build
1. Create TranscribeMedicalService replacing DeepgramService
2. Use /get-credentials Lambda for temporary AWS credentials
3. Stream audio to Transcribe Medical WebSocket
4. Handle interim and final results
5. Compare quality with Deepgram

## Acceptance criteria
- Real-time transcription works with Transcribe Medical
- Quality acceptable for clinical dictation
- No Deepgram API calls in production mode
- Fallback available if Transcribe is unavailable

milestones.title: Fix client-side punctuation (smart_format disabled)
milestones.description: The smart_format parameter is disabled for Deepgram, causing poor punctuation in transcription output. Either re-enable smart_format (without dictation mode) or add client-side punctuation rules.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the sevaro-scribe repo, fix punctuation in transcription output. Re-enable smart_format or add client-side punctuation rules.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Fix Punctuation

**Repo:** sevaro-scribe

## Problem
smart_format is disabled, causing poor punctuation. Note: dictation=true suppresses punctuate=true (known Deepgram bug).

## Fix options
1. Enable smart_format=true without dictation mode
2. Add client-side punctuation rules
3. Post-process with AI cleanup

## Acceptance criteria
- Proper sentence capitalization and periods
- Medical terms correctly formatted
- No regression in transcription accuracy

milestones.title: Steel man test remediations (logging, thread safety, CRC32C, dead code)
milestones.description: Address findings from the steel man test: improve logging consistency, fix thread safety issues, validate CRC32C checksums, and remove dead code paths.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the VoiceTranscriber repo, address steel man test findings: fix logging consistency, thread safety issues, CRC32C validation, and remove dead code.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Steel Man Remediations

**Repo:** VoiceTranscriber

## Findings to address
1. Logging: Inconsistent log levels, some debug logs in release builds
2. Thread safety: UI updates from background threads in some paths
3. CRC32C: Checksum validation not verified in all upload paths
4. Dead code: Unused functions and obsolete feature flags

## Acceptance criteria
- All logs use consistent format and appropriate levels
- No UI updates from background threads (use @MainActor)
- CRC32C validated in all data paths
- Dead code removed
- Build succeeds with no warnings

milestones.title: Evaluate AWS Transcribe Medical vs Deepgram for production
milestones.description: Compare AWS Transcribe Medical and Deepgram Nova-3 for production transcription quality. Test with the shared test audio corpus to measure accuracy, latency, and medical term recognition.
milestones.quickPrompt: Evaluate AWS Transcribe Medical vs Deepgram Nova-3 for VoiceTranscriber production use. Test with ~/dev/test-audio/ corpus and compare accuracy, latency, and medical terms.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Transcription Service Evaluation

**Repo:** VoiceTranscriber

## Test plan
1. Run all test audio files through both services
2. Measure: word error rate, latency, medical term accuracy
3. Compare cost per hour of audio
4. Document findings

## Test corpus
~/dev/test-audio/ — 16kHz 16-bit PCM WAV files
Includes: clinical dictation, encounters, ER scenarios, elderly speakers

## Decision criteria
- Medical term accuracy is most important
- Latency under 500ms for streaming
- Cost-effective at projected volume
- HIPAA compliance mandatory for production
manualSteps: Run test audio through Deepgram Nova-3
Run same audio through Transcribe Medical

milestones.title: Cloud sync backend (iCloud or API TBD)
milestones.description: Add cloud sync so journal moments persist across devices. Decision needed: iCloud (simpler, Apple-only) or custom API backend (cross-platform potential).
milestones.quickPrompt: Add cloud sync to PostSeed. Decide between iCloud (simpler) or custom API backend, then implement. Start with iCloud unless cross-platform is needed.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Cloud Sync

**Repo:** PostSeed

## Options
1. iCloud + CloudKit — automatic sync, no server, Apple-only
2. Custom API + RDS — full control, cross-platform, more work

## Recommendation: Start with iCloud
- Simpler implementation
- No server costs
- Automatic conflict resolution
- Can add API backend later if needed

## Build (iCloud path)
1. Enable CloudKit capability
2. Create CKRecord types for Moments
3. Sync on-device data with CloudKit
4. Handle conflicts and offline mode

## Acceptance criteria
- Moments sync across iPhone and iPad
- Offline capture works (syncs when online)
- No data loss on conflict

4. Combine search and filters

- Search finds moments by transcription content
- Filters narrow journal view
- Performance good with 100+ moments
